mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS over the last quarter lowers the discount rate on equities and eases financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when policy is moving toward easing.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-month window but subsequent 20-day SPX returns are negative in more than 60% of cases, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and demand, which pressures earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of UNRATE rise-triggered instances, or if the triggered sample shows no negative edge versus unconditional returns.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in PAYEMS and RSAFS indicates broadening labor income and retail demand, which supports revenue growth expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal of rising PAYEMS and RSAFS does not outperform either proxy alone, or if triggered periods fail to deliver positive excess 20-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO confirms industrial output expansion and stronger real activity, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are not positive after INDPRO uptrends, or if the sign relation reverses in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST rises sharply from a depressed level, housing activity is recovering and interest-sensitive demand is stabilizing, which supports equities and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by at least 8% over 2 months after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing recovery triggers are followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if the average return is below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS rate combined with elevated CPIAUCSL implies restrictive real rates and inflation pressure, which compresses equity valuations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS above 4.5% while CPIAUCSL is still rising over the last 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the restrictive-rate/inflation regime does not produce lower 20-day SPX returns than either condition alone, or if the sign is positive in most triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A low UNRATE with continued PAYEMS growth signals resilient labor markets and stronger consumption capacity, which supports earnings and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined labor-strength signal fails to deliver positive 5-day returns relative to unconditional returns, or if the effect disappears after controlling for each proxy separately.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising RSAFS together with rising GDPC1 signals that nominal spending and real output are both accelerating, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in RSAFS and GDPC1 do not correspond to higher 60-day SPX returns than either series alone, or if the signal is negative in a majority of matched observations.
